blocks.content: At some point between the second act and the third, he stopped watching the movie entirely.

He was aware of the screen and the sound, but mostly he was aware of Daisy — her elbows on her knees, chin in one hand, the projected light moving across her face. There was something about that particular light.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You're doing the thing where you think you're not.
He didn't have an answer for that. Not yet.
She laughed — quietly, just for him — and looked back at the screen.

When the movie ended the park came back to itself — lights, noise, people standing and folding blankets. They found Eli still asleep on his half of the blanket, and it took three attempts and a gentle shake from Daisy to bring him back to the surface.

blocks.speaker: Eli
blocks.content: I rested my eyes. There's a difference.
